skill.name: grimoire-polymarket
skill.description: Queries Polymarket market discovery data, CLOB market state, and authenticated order-management operations through the Grimoire venue CLI wrapper.
skill.summary: Use Grimoire to query Polymarket markets, CLOB data, and order state.
skill.author: franalgaba

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution findings, but they are Markdown command examples and CLI names rather than Ruby shell backtick execution. The skill still has medium risk because it directs agents to run external Polymarket/Grimoire CLIs and use private key or API credentials for authenticated trading operations.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to invoke Grimoire, npx, bun, and the Polymarket CLI. This is expected for the skill, but it can execute local binaries, install packages, and interact with live market systems.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly lists external CLI invocations and installation commands. The use appears intentional and domain-appropriate, but the operational risk is clear.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Trading Credentials Referenced
medium_findings.description: The skill documents a required private key and optional Polymarket API credentials for authenticated CLOB operations. Exposure or misuse of these credentials could enable account actions or financial loss.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable names explicitly include a private key, API key, API secret, and passphrase. These credentials are used for authenticated order-management operations described by the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines are natural language and command terminology. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptographic implementation was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain description text, CLOB wording, aliases, and market/trade commands. There is no executable cryptographic code in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick findings are Markdown inline code spans and fenced command examples. They are not executable Ruby code, but they still document commands that an agent may run.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and uses backticks for formatting. The command examples are not code executed by the skill file itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Surface
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on external CLI execution for market queries and order-management workflows. Users should review commands before execution and avoid untrusted parameters.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command surface is explicitly documented and includes trading-related CLI operations. It is a legitimate capability with elevated operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Environment Variables
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires or supports environment variables containing private keys and API credentials. These secrets must not be exposed in prompts, logs, or shared terminal output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential variable names are explicit and include private key and secret values. No exfiltration code was found, but secret handling risk remains.

content.user_title: Query Polymarket Markets and CLOB Data
content.value_statement: Polymarket workflows often require several CLI commands and careful filtering. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused command surface for discovery, CLOB data, and order checks.
actual_capabilities: Runs Grimoire Polymarket venue commands for market discovery and adapter health checks.
Searches markets by query, slug, question, event, tag, category, league, sport, and status filters.
Retrieves CLOB market data such as books, midpoint prices, side prices, spread, and trade history.
Supports authenticated reads for orders, trades, balances, and allowance-related CLOB state.
Documents allowed passthrough command groups and blocked high-risk wallet or shell groups.
limitations: Requires the Grimoire CLI wrapper and the official Polymarket CLI to be installed or callable.
Authenticated workflows require private keys or Polymarket API credentials in environment variables.
Order-management commands can affect live trading accounts and need explicit user review.
The skill is not financial advice and does not validate market outcomes or trading strategy quality.

use_cases.title: Research Active Markets
use_cases.description: Find open and active Polymarket markets by topic, category, event, league, or sport for structured research.

use_cases.title: Inspect CLOB State
use_cases.description: Check books, midpoint prices, spreads, recent trades, and balances before making an operational decision.

use_cases.title: Automate Market Data Checks
use_cases.description: Use JSON output from the venue adapter in scripts, notebooks, and agent workflows that monitor market conditions.

prompt_templates.title: Find Markets by Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Polymarket skill to search for active open markets about bitcoin. Return a concise table with market question, slug, status, and identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Compare CLOB Prices
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Polymarket CLOB commands to inspect the book, midpoint, buy price, sell price, and spread for this token ID: <token_id>.

prompt_templates.title: Check Authenticated Order State
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the configured Polymarket credentials, list open orders and recent trades for this condition ID: <condition_id>. Do not place or cancel orders.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Research Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a repeatable workflow that discovers active sports markets, filters for tradable markets, checks CLOB depth, and summarizes the strongest liquidity candidates.

output_examples.input: Search active Polymarket markets for bitcoin.
output_examples.output: A short table of matching market questions, slugs, active/open status, and identifiers suitable for follow-up CLOB queries.

output_examples.input: Inspect the CLOB book and midpoint for a token ID.
output_examples.output: A concise market-state summary with best bid, best ask, midpoint, spread, depth notes, and any command errors.

output_examples.input: List open orders for a condition ID without trading.
output_examples.output: A read-only account summary showing open order counts, relevant order details, and whether credentials were available.

best_practices: Run the doctor and info commands before market or account operations.
Use JSON output for automation and keep raw command output out of shared prompts when it may contain secrets.
Require explicit user confirmation before any order placement, cancellation, or credential-dependent action.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private keys, API secrets, or passphrases into chat prompts or issue reports.
Do not run wallet, bridge, approval, setup, upgrade, or shell commands through this skill.
Do not treat market data summaries as financial advice or as a substitute for independent verification.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It uses the Grimoire Polymarket venue adapter and the official Polymarket CLI for market and CLOB operations.

faq.question: Can it place or cancel orders?
faq.answer: The documented surface includes order-management operations, so users should require explicit confirmation before any trading action.

faq.question: Does it need credentials?
faq.answer: Market discovery can be read-only, but authenticated CLOB operations require a private key or Polymarket API credentials.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses external commands and sensitive trading credentials.

faq.question: Does it call Polymarket HTTP APIs directly?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to stay on the CLI surface and not call Polymarket HTTP APIs directly from advisory tools.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
